raw_text:
I need to classify this response using only the letter codes provided.

Let me analyze the response against the codes:

A = greater empathy/compassion
B = better truth-seeking/changing minds
C = better disagreement/less polarization
D = inequality/justice/rights
E = reduce poverty/material deprivation
F = basic needs/material floor
G = education/critical thinking
H = climate/environment
I = health/disease
J = reduce war/violence
K = better institutions/governance
L = anti-self-deception/anti-tribalism
M = felt interconnection/less separateness
N = dehumanization/distance reduction
O = reduce suffering/pain
P = technology/AI safety
Q = epistemic humility/uncertainty tolerance
R = other world-change wish

The response emphasizes:
- "make empathy a physical sense" → A (greater empathy/compassion)
- "felt interconnection" / "your choices on other people with the same immediacy as your own hunger" → M (felt interconnection/less separateness)
- "dehumanization" / "the quiet violence of indifference" → N (dehumanization/distance reduction)
- "What does this cost us?" / shift from "What can I get away with?" → K (better institutions/governance) and/or L (anti-self-deception)
- "justice, sustainability, mutual care" → D (inequality/justice/rights), H (climate/environment)
- "reduce suffering/pain" → O

The core themes are empathy as visceral (A, M), reducing dehumanization/distance (N), and shifting incentives/institutions (K).

Most prominent: A, M, N, K

A M N K
